meaning: <美>n.奇卡诺人(指墨西哥裔美国人或在美国的讲西班牙语的拉丁美洲人后裔)
examples: They developed collectives and established cultural centers that functioned as the public-relations arm of the Chicano sociopolitical movement.
Such community engagement is characteristic of the Chicano art movement as a whole, which evolved from the same foundations as the Chicano civil rights movement of the mid-1960s.
I just say I'm a Chicano, for example, because my parents are from Mexico.
Latinos hardly fit into the embattled Chicano stereotype once so cherished by the left.
There are no scholars like there are for the Chicano movement in California.
